SnowDistribution: Generally firm snowpack with areas of softer windblown snow. Good visibility with some sunshine. Light winds approx 10mph.
Comment: Cold and dry into the weekend.
WeatherInfluences: Remaining dry with cloud clearing to be generally bright and sunny through the day. Feeling cold with Northerly winds easing, fresh to moderate. The freezing level staying around 300 metres.
SnowStability: The old snowpack will be generally frozen, hard and stable, with small pockets of newer windblown snow in sheltered locations. Above 900 metres, areas of unstable windslab will be present on mainly South-West to North-West aspects. These accumulations are not widespread but will remain weakly bonded and lie on a fragile thin crust. Small cornices will remain present on steep Westerly aspects of the highest summits. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Dry and bright with cloud clearing through the day and periods of sunshine. Very cold around the summits, about minus 5 Celsius, with a light to moderate Easterly wind.
ObservedSnowStability: The old snowpack is generally frozen, hard and stable, with small pockets of fresh windblown snow in sheltered locations. Above 900 metres, areas of unstable windslab are present on mainly South-West to North-West aspects. Whilst these accumulations are not widespread they are weakly bonded and are lying on a fragile thin crust. Cornices are present on Westerly aspects of the highest summits. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Very hard snow ice on exposed windward slopes.  Good visibility.  Winds 20-30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts
Comment: Areas exposed to recent strong winds have become very icy.
WeatherInfluences: Remaining cold at all levels with light snowfall expected, mainly overnight. Winds will be fresh, some North-North-East but backing to the North then North-North-West early afternoon.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will persist in wind sheltered locations mainly on South-West to North-West aspects above 900 metres. Isolated unstable deposits will also be found on  South-East, South and North aspects and at lower elevations where any steep sheltered features will be most affected. Stability of newly formed cornices will be poor. Exposed locations will be icy.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A bright day with some broken cloud cover around the tops. A temperature of -5 degrees was recorded at 900 metres. There was a strong breeze from the East-North-East.
ObservedSnowStability: Poorly bonded windslab was present in wind sheltered locations mainly on South-West to North-West aspects above 900 metres. Localised windslab is also found on both South and North aspects and at lower levels. Fragile cornices are forming. Wind exposed slopes are scoured and very icy.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Snowpack mostly very firm. Mostly good visibility. Winds 10 - 20 mph.
Comment: The older snowpack will be frozen and very firm.
WeatherInfluences: Moderate North-East winds overnight back to the North-North-West during the day. It will be cold with occasional snow showers.
SnowStability: The older snowpack will remain firm and stable. Any windslab accumulations are expected to be isolated, shallow and easily avoidable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry day with some sunshine and fresh East-North-Easterly winds. The freezing level was around 400 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: In most places the snowpack is firm, frozen and stable. Isolated areas of shallow moderately bonded windslab are present mainly on South-West and West aspects above 700 metres. These areas are generally avoidable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Good snow cover, wind scoured and icy on some aspects.  Good visibility for most of the day with occasional spells of cloud sweeping across the highest tops.  Winds 20-30 mph at higher levels.
Comment: Wind exposed areas are very firm and icy.
WeatherInfluences: Light snowfall is expected to clear by early morning. Fresh East-North-East winds will gradually back to become North-North-Westerly through the afternoon. The freezing level will be around 400 metres.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will develop on South-East to South-West aspects above 900m. Widespread unstable windslab will remain on South-West through West to North aspects; more localised on North-East aspects. Coire headwalls, crag aprons and steep slopes especially affected. At lower elevations localised unstable windslab will be present on some steep banks and burn lines. Elsewhere above 1000m weaknesses will remain beneath a widespread friable crust. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Light snowfall cleared to leave a dry day with fresh Easterly winds. The midday freezing level was around 500 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Drifting snow at higher levels with unstable windslab developing on South-West, through West to North aspects above 900 metres. Cross loading has affected North-East aspects. Coire headwalls, crag aprons and steep convex terrain are most affected. At lower elevations localised instabilities are present on steep banks and burn lines. Elsewhere above 1000 metres weaknesses remain present beneath a widespread friable crust. Cornices are fragile.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Good snow cover above 700 metres, mostly pretty firm and icy. Some cloud on the summits this morning, but cold and clear this afternoon.  Winds 20-30 mph easing through the day.
Comment: Many areas scoured by the strong winds and will be firm and icy. However, patches of windslab remain.
WeatherInfluences: A pleasant dry day with some sunshine and a North-Easterly breeze. The freezing level will be around 300 metres.
SnowStability: Little change is expected in the snowpack. In general it will remain hard, icy and stable. However, some areas of moderately bonded windslab will remain. These will be mainly in hollows and wind sheltered locations on South-West through West to North aspects above 1000 metres. These will not be particularly extensive, and will generally be avoidable.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: After a little overnight snow it was a dry day with some pleasant afternoon sunshine. The Easterly wind eased. The freezing level was about 500 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: In general the snowpack is firm icy and stable. There are some deposits of moderately bonded windslab in sheltered South-West through West to North aspects above 1000 metres. However, these tend not to be particularly extensive. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Very hard snow ice on exposed windward slopes. Good visibility.  Winds approx 10 - 20 mph fresh, cooling.
Comment: Cold North-Easterly airflow for the next couple of days. A slip or fall could have serious consequences due to hard snowpack and exposed rocks.
WeatherInfluences: Mostly dry with the freezing level around 400 metres. Fresh East-North-East winds will back North-North-West and steadily decrease to become moderate.
SnowStability: The snowpack will be mostly firm and icy with good stability all areas. Isolated accumulations of windslab and softer deposits will be present above 900 metres on South-West through West to North-West aspects. These will be generally shallow and not extensive. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A dry and bright day with the freezing level around 500 metres. Fresh to moderate East-North-Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is mostly firm, icy and stable in all areas. Isolated accumulations of windslab and softer deposits exist primarily in wind-sheltered locations above 900 metres on South-West through West to North-West aspects. These are generally shallow and avoidable. The avalanche hazard is Low.
